corrections.description: Missing the actual Christmas poem by Alfred Lambourne that should start the article
corrections.text: Ah! as the world awakens to the morn, The risen sun illumes the mountain domes, As comes the day on which the Christ was born, What prayer will rise from out now saddened homes? What from the sweetheart in the spring of life, With streaming eyes and words all sobbing wild? And from the trembling lips of some fond wife, To look this day upon her new-born child? Or from that one who hides her bitter woe, Who mourns her last-born son, now far away. The widowed mother with her locks of snow, Who all subdued doth bow her head to pray? Ah, Christ, in tenderness all ones above, Heal these three hearts with Thy eternal love!

corrections.description: Contains content from next article about picture gallery
Contains both poem and Red Cross Conference article - should only contain the poem
corrections.text: THE CLOSE OF THE YEAR. Edith McClenden. The year is drawing to its close. With all its hopes and fears; Tt brought us many sorrows. Tt brought us many tears. It brought us many pleasures; It brought us joys as well; It brought unnumbered blessings, Yes, more than we can tell. And now that it is ending Let each one try to see How very kind and thankful For all this, we can 1m?. And as each year is passing, Let's count our blessing over; We'll find they've brought us greater joys Than we ever knew before.
The Red Cross Conference in Denver. Amy Brown Lyman.

corrections.description: Missing proper start of Home Science Department article
corrections.text: Home Science Department. Janctte A. Hyde. DEMONSTRATIONS TO BE USED WITH LESSON ON POOD VALUES. These suggestions are made as supplementary to the I Ionic Science lesson in our Guide. A. A table set with samples of food in amounts to agree with cuts in lesson. Each sample to be accompanied with a card denoting food values. See lesson table. B. Demonstrations of meals for children. C. Members of class to bring one article containing a hundred calorie portion. D. A sample meal of poor combinations, either too much starch or too much protein. A meal showing the correct combinations.

notes: Many files contain index/table of contents material instead of actual article content. Several articles appear to be missing entirely from the extraction. The OCR quality seems good for the articles that are properly extracted, but there are significant gaps in content coverage.
